summary: bin/rails test cannot run at all: two test files added/modified for the live pipeline status feature (test/services/pipelines/broadcast_status_test.rb and test/services/phases/manager_tick_test.rb) raise `NameError: uninitialized constant Turbo::Broadcastable::TestHelper` at load time. Because Minitest loads every *_test.rb file before running any test, this one error aborts the entire suite -- none of the other 96+ tests (which all pass individually/in the rest of the suite) can execute via the standard `bin/rails test` command. rubocop is clean (115 files, no offenses).

findings.issue: `include Turbo::Broadcastable::TestHelper` at the class level raises `NameError: uninitialized constant Turbo::Broadcastable::TestHelper`, which aborts the entire `bin/rails test` run (Minitest loads all test files up front, so this single load error prevents every other test in the suite from running). Root cause: turbo-rails only requires/includes this module via a nested `ActiveSupport.on_load(:action_cable) { on_load(:active_support_test_case) { require "turbo/broadcastable/test_helper"; include Turbo::Broadcastable::TestHelper } }` hook, and the outer `:action_cable` hook only fires once `ActionCable::Server::Base` is loaded -- which never happens in this app (no app/channels directory, no `mount ActionCable.server` in config/routes.rb, and config.eager_load is false in test unless ENV['CI'] is set). Verified directly: `Turbo::Broadcastable::TestHelper` is undefined even after fully loading test_helper.rb/Rails in this environment. The codebase's pre-existing, working pattern for asserting turbo broadcasts (test/services/step_runs/broadcast_card_test.rb) instead uses `ActiveJob::TestHelper#assert_enqueued_with(job: Turbo::Streams::ActionBroadcastJob)` and never references `Turbo::Broadcastable::TestHelper`/`capture_turbo_stream_broadcasts` -- this new test deviates from that established pattern and hits a real, reproducible gap in this app's ActionCable wiring.

Same root cause as F1: `include Turbo::Broadcastable::TestHelper` (added this iteration) plus the `capture_turbo_stream_broadcasts`-based `pipeline_broadcast_targets` helper raise the identical `NameError` at load time, independently aborting the suite even if F1 were fixed on its own.
